name: Absolute Monarchy
description: Unrestricted personal sovereignty; no institutional checks on royal power

name: Monarchy
description: Hereditary rule with some institutional constraints (council, assembly, custom)

name: Feudal Monarchy
description: Monarch atop feudal hierarchy; vassals hold real territorial power

name: Constitutional Monarchy
description: Monarchy limited by constitution and/or parliament

name: Elective Monarchy
description: Monarch chosen by designated electors (e.g., HRE, Poland-Lithuania)

name: Imperial Monarchy
description: Multi-ethnic empire under emperor; centralized but governing diverse peoples

name: Divine Monarchy
description: Ruler considered divine or direct agent of deity (pharaohs, Khmer devaraja)

name: Chinese Imperial
description: Mandate of Heaven; Confucian bureaucracy; civil examination system

name: Sultanate
description: Islamic monarchy; sultan as sovereign wielding temporal authority

name: Caliphate
description: Islamic universal sovereignty; caliph as both political and religious leader

name: Emirate
description: Islamic principality; emir as regional sovereign or governor

name: Imamate
description: Shia or Ibadi theocratic sovereignty; imam as divinely guided leader

name: Khaganate
description: Steppe supreme sovereignty; khagan rules confederation of tribes through personal authority

name: Khanate
description: Steppe regional sovereignty; khan as hereditary or elected tribal sovereign

name: Shogunate
description: Japanese military government; shogun holds de facto power while emperor reigns

name: Principality
description: Small sovereign domain ruled by prince, grand duke, or equivalent

name: Theocracy
description: Rule by religious authority; political legitimacy derived from divine mandate

name: Republic
description: Non-hereditary institutional rule; power held by elected or appointed officials

name: Democratic Republic
description: Elected representative government; popular sovereignty through universal suffrage

name: Oligarchy
description: Rule by small elite group; power concentrated in wealthy or aristocratic families

name: City-State
description: Independent city-based polity; often with assembly or council governance

name: Communist State
description: Marxist-Leninist one-party state; party claims vanguard legitimacy

name: Tribal Confederation
description: Alliance of tribal or clan groups; collective leadership through council of chiefs

name: Confederation
description: Loose union of sovereign entities; central authority limited by member consent

name: Colonial Administration
description: Territory administered by foreign imperial power; no indigenous sovereignty

name: Puppet State
description: Nominal sovereignty; de facto controlled by external power
